name: Aglio e Olio
description: A very garlicky, restaurant-level aglio e olio using garlic three ways: mellow blended garlic purée, slow-infused garlic oil, and crispy garlic chips.
recipeNotes: The sauce should be glossy and lightly creamy, not oily and separated.
Boiled and blended garlic gives the pasta a sweet, mellow garlic backbone.
Slow-infused garlic oil gives the classic aglio e olio aroma.
Crispy garlic chips add texture and a sharper garlic finish.
Lemon zest helps keep the dish bright and balanced.
recipeIngredient: 1/2 tsp red pepper flakes, or more to taste
2–3 tbsp grated Parmigiano or Pecorino, optional

recipeInstructions.name: Make the mellow garlic purée
recipeInstructions.text: Peel 8–10 garlic cloves. Put them in a small pot, cover with water, and simmer gently for 8–10 minutes, until very soft. Drain, then blend or mash with 1 tablespoon olive oil and a pinch of salt. Later, loosen with 2–3 tablespoons hot pasta water as needed until smooth and pourable.

recipeInstructions.name: Make the garlic chips and garlic oil
recipeInstructions.text: Thinly slice the remaining 4–6 garlic cloves. Add the olive oil and sliced garlic to a cold pan. Turn the heat to low and cook slowly until the garlic turns pale golden, about 5–8 minutes. Remove the garlic chips with a spoon and set them aside. Keep the infused oil in the pan. Do not let the garlic turn dark brown or it may become bitter.

recipeInstructions.name: Cook the pasta
recipeInstructions.text: Boil the pasta in well-salted water until 1 minute shy of al dente. Before draining, reserve at least 1 cup of pasta water.

recipeInstructions.name: Start the sauce
recipeInstructions.text: Return the pan with the garlic oil to low heat. Add the red pepper flakes, garlic purée, and 1/2 cup reserved pasta water. Stir until the purée loosens into the oil and pasta water.

recipeInstructions.name: Emulsify with pasta
recipeInstructions.text: Add the pasta directly into the pan. Toss vigorously over medium-low heat, adding splashes of pasta water as needed, until the sauce becomes glossy, clingy, and lightly creamy.

recipeInstructions.name: Finish and serve
recipeInstructions.text: Finish with parsley, lemon zest, a tiny squeeze of lemon juice, black pepper, optional butter, and optional cheese. Taste and adjust salt. Top with the crispy garlic chips before serving.
